demographics.living_situation: lives in an apartment with a roommate

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My alarm clock went off really loudly this morning, and the next thing I remember is waking up on the floor with my roommate shaking me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I passed out with no warning right when the loud alarm went off, and it really scared me — I want to know what happened and whether it could happen again.

symptom_narrative.onset: this morning, the instant a very loud alarm went off
symptom_narrative.duration: the blackout itself lasted well under a minute; I feel back to normal now
symptom_narrative.character: I dropped suddenly with no warning; my roommate says I stiffened and twitched for a few seconds and then came round
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really frightening — I've never blacked out like this before and I feel shaken
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it happened once, right after the loud noise
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: I came round on my own within about a minute and feel okay now
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the sudden loud alarm seemed to set it off
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest pain and no racing or pounding heart before it happened; just the loud noise and then I was on the floor
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never fainted or blacked out before that I can remember
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: fully recovered and able to do everything, but anxious it might happen again

family_history_as_reported.narrative: My mum's sister — my aunt — died suddenly at 25, and no one ever really knew why.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Lives with a roommate who saw the whole thing happen and is with me now for this call.

objective_data_offered.value: roommate says she looks back to normal now — alert, talking clearly, normal colour, sitting on the couch
objective_data_offered.timing: now, a few minutes after the collapse

objective_data_offered.value: roommate reports she suddenly dropped to the floor, went stiff and twitched for about 10 seconds, then woke up within a minute and knew where she was; no lasting confusion noticed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: bystander account of a brief event; timing is approximate

objective_data_offered.value: the building fire alarm is still going off on and off — it's being tested intermittently
